le","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"paused","type":"bool"}],"name":"setGlobalPause","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes4","name":"interfaceId","type":"bytes4"}],"name":"supportsInterface","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"id","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"active","type":"bool"}],"name":"toggleAirdrop","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"usedRoots","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"id","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"allocation","type":"uint256"}],"name":"vestedAmount","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"stateMutability":"payable","type":"receive"}],"devdoc":{"author":"FlashVerse","details":"Security model \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 \u2022 Strict Checks-Effects-Interactions (CEI) in EVERY claim path. \u2022 ReentrancyGuard on all external state-mutating functions. \u2022 Per-airdrop ID uniqueness enforced via `exists` bitmap. \u2022 Per-airdrop Merkle-root uniqueness enforced via `usedRoots` bitmap. \u2022 Leaf pre-image includes (account, allocation, nonce, category) so each wallet has exactly one leaf per airdrop; replay is impossible. \u2022 Nonce-per-airdrop-per-account is incremented on every successful claim, so old proofs are invalidated automatically. \u2022 BatchClaim enforces CEI correctly: all state is written BEFORE any external token transfer. \u2022 Duplicate airdrop IDs within a single batch call are detected and rejected in the validation phase, preventing double-spend. \u2022 `tokens` parameter removed from batchClaim; token address is always read from the immutable on-chain Airdrop struct. \u2022 emergencyWithdraw is protected by both ADMIN_ROLE and nonReentrant.Vesting formula \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 before cliff  \u2192 0 after full duration \u2192 100 % between cliff and duration \u2192 linear interpolation over elapsed time from `start` (not from cliff end).","errors":{"AccessControlBadConfirmation()":[{"details":"The caller of a function is not the expected one. NOTE: Don't confuse with {AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount}."}],"AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount(address,bytes32)":[{"details":"The `account` is missing a role."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}],"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"events":{"RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted to signal this."},"RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}."},"RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)"}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"batchClaim(uint256[],uint256[],uint256[],uint256[],bytes32[][])":{"details":"Strict CEI is maintained: 1. ALL checks (proof, nonce, existence, active, vested > claimed). 2. Detect duplicate IDs in the batch (prevent double-spend). 3. Compute claimable amounts and aggregate by token. 4. Write ALL state changes (claimed, nonces) and emit events. 5. Execute ALL transfers last. The `tokens` parameter present in the original implementation has been removed; the token address is always sourced from the on-chain struct.","params":{"allocations":"Merkle-leaf allocations (parallel to ids).","categories":"Merkle-leaf categories (parallel to ids).","ids":"Airdrop IDs to claim from (no duplicates allowed).","noncesArr":"Merkle-leaf nonces (parallel to ids).","proofs":"Merkle proofs (parallel to ids)."}},"claim(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes32[])":{"params":{"allocation":"Total allocation encoded in the Merkle leaf.","category":"Category encoded in the Merkle leaf (e.g., team, public, etc.).","id":"Airdrop to claim from.","nonce":"Nonce encoded in the Merkle leaf (must match on-chain nonce).","proof":"Merkle proof for the leaf (account, allocation, nonce, category)."}},"createAirdrop(uint256,address,bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Both `id` and `merkleRoot` are globally unique across all airdrops.","params":{"cliffSeconds":"Seconds after `start` before any tokens are claimable.","durationSeconds":"Total length of the vesting window in seconds. Pass 0 for instant (no vesting); cliff must then also be 0.","id":"Unique numeric identifier for this airdrop (\u2265 1).","merkleRoot":"Root of the Merkle tree; must never have been used before.","start":"Unix timestamp at which vesting begins (must be \u2265 block.timestamp).","token":"ERC-20 token address, or address(0) for native ETH.","totalAllocated":"Total tokens available for this airdrop (informational)."}},"emergencyWithdraw(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Should only be used if funds become stranded (e.g., a bad airdrop config)."},"getClaimableAmount(uint256,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes32[])":{"params":{"account":"Wallet to check.","allocation":"Leaf allocation for this account.","category":"Leaf category for this account.","id":"Airdrop identifier.","nonce":"Leaf nonce for this account.","proof":"Merkle proof."},"returns":{"_0":"Claimable amount (0 if proof invalid, paused, or nothing new vested)."}},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas."},"vestedAmount(uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"allocation":"Total allocation for the querying account.","id":"Airdrop identifier."},"returns":{"_0":"Amount that has vested so far (before subtracting already-claimed)."}}},"title":"ClaimModule","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"airdropIds(uint256)":{"notice":"Ordered list of all registered airdrop IDs (for off-chain transaction."},"claim(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes32[])":{"notice":"Claim newly vested tokens for a single airdrop."},"claimed(uint256,address)":{"notice":"airdropId \u2192 account \u2192 total tokens already transferred"},"createAirdrop(uint256,address,bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Register a new airdrop."},"emergencyWithdraw(address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Withdraw any tokens or ETH from this contract in an emergency."},"exists(uint256)":{"notice":"airdropId \u2192 true if it has been created"},"getAirdropIds()":{"notice":"Returns the full list of registered airdrop IDs."},"getClaimableAmount(uint256,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes32[])":{"notice":"Returns how many tokens `account` can claim right now for airdrop `id`."},"nonces(uint256,address)":{"notice":"airdropId \u2192 account \u2192 current nonce (increments on every claim)"},"setGlobalPause(bool)":{"notice":"Pause or unpause all claim operations globally."},"toggleAirdrop(uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Enable or disable a specific airdrop without destroying its data."},"usedRoots(bytes32)":{"notice":"merkleRoot \u2192 true if already used in any airdrop"},"vestedAmount(uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Returns the vested portion of `allocation` for airdrop `id` batchClaim(uint256[],uint256[],uint256[].details: Strict CEI is maintained: 1. ALL checks (proof, nonce, existence, active, vested > claimed). 2. Detect duplicate IDs in the batch (prevent double-spend). 3. Compute claimable amounts and aggregate by token. 4. Write ALL state changes (claimed, nonces) and emit events. 5. Execute ALL transfers last. The `tokens` parameter present in the original implementation has been removed; the token address is always sourced from the on-chain struct.

params.ids: Airdrop IDs to claim from (no duplicates allowed).

params.allocation: Total allocation encoded in the Merkle leaf.
params.category: Category encoded in the Merkle leaf (e.g., team, public, etc.).
params.nonce: Nonce encoded in the Merkle leaf (must match on-chain nonce).
params.proof: Merkle proof for the leaf (account, allocation, nonce, category).

createAirdrop(uint256,address,bytes32,ui.details: Both `id` and `merkleRoot` are globally unique across all airdrops.

params.cliffSeconds: Seconds after `start` before any tokens are claimable.
params.durationSeconds: Total length of the vesting window in seconds. Pass 0 for instant (no vesting); cliff must then also be 0.
params.id: Unique numeric identifier for this airdrop (≥ 1).
params.merkleRoot: Root of the Merkle tree; must never have been used before.
params.start: Unix timestamp at which vesting begins (must be ≥ block.timestamp).
params.token: ERC-20 token address, or address(0) for native ETH.
params.totalAllocated: Total tokens available for this airdrop (informational).

emergencyWithdraw(address,address,uint25.details: Should only be used if funds become stranded (e.g., a bad airdrop config).

returns._0: Claimable amount (0 if proof invalid, paused, or nothing new vested).

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

params.allocation: Total allocation for the querying account.

returns._0: Amount that has vested so far (before subtracting already-claimed).

airdropIds(uint256).notice: Ordered list of all registered airdrop IDs (for off-chain indexing)

batchClaim(uint256[],uint256[],uint256[].notice: Claim from multiple airdrops in a single transaction.

claim(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,by.notice: Claim newly vested tokens for a single airdrop.

claimed(uint256,address).notice: airdropId → account → total tokens already transferred

emergencyWithdraw(address,address,uint25.notice: Withdraw any tokens or ETH from this contract in an emergency.

exists(uint256).notice: airdropId → true if it has been created

getAirdropIds().notice: Returns the full list of registered airdrop IDs.

getClaimableAmount(uint256,address,uint2.notice: Returns how many tokens `account` can claim right now for airdrop `id`.

nonces(uint256,address).notice: airdropId → account → current nonce (increments on every claim)

setGlobalPause(bool).notice: Pause or unpause all claim operations globally.

toggleAirdrop(uint256,bool).notice: Enable or disable a specific airdrop without destroying its data.

usedRoots(bytes32).notice: merkleRoot → true if already used in any airdrop

vestedAmount(uint256,uint256).notice: Returns the vested portion of `allocation` for airdrop `id` at the current block timestamp.
